title: mirabilis laevis var. aspera
intro: Mirabilis laevis var. aspera, also known as rough water-willow or rough marsh-willow, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its purple flowers and distinctive, rough leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Mirabilis laevis var. aspera include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and alleviate symptoms of fever and colds. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk remedies and herbal supplements to promote overall well-being.
